codeReview.summary: Feature ran full validation suite and created validation-state.json with pass status for all VAL-CONTENT-* assertions. 956 content validation assertions passed, lint passed with 4 non-blocking warnings. All 10 lectures present with substantial content, no duplicates, no orphaned sections.

issues.description: Source provenance mismatch: L5.provenance.sourceFile='Lecture 6.pdf' but lecture5_full.txt exists in data/raw/ and feature 'enhance-l5-full' extracted from lecture5_full.txt. L5 should source from 'Lecture 5.pdf' or lecture5_full.txt. Similarly L6 also sources from Lecture 6.pdf but lecture6_full.txt exists.

validate-content.ts does NOT validate lectures.json content. It validates: types.ts, instruction-index.json, concept-taxonomy.json, question-bank.json, course-map.json, data integrity, and cross-references. The VAL-CONTENT-* assertions (lecture content, char counts, duplicates, orphans, provenance) are NOT validated by this script - they appear to be manually/separately verified and written to validation-state.json.

VAL-CONTENT-007 claims 'All 10 lectures have provenance.sourceFile matching raw PDF files'. While PDFs exist in data/raw/, L5 source mismatch (see blocking issue) means the provenance does NOT match the actual extraction source.

sharedStateObservations.observation: validation-worker skill procedure says to run 'npm run validate:content' but the script doesn't validate lectures.json content. The VAL-CONTENT-* assertions (001-009) covering lecture content, char counts, duplicates, orphans, and provenance are NOT produced by the script - they were manually added to validation-state.json.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: validate-content.ts (956 assertions) covers types/instructions/taxonomy/question-bank/course-map/data-integrity/cross-refs but NOT lectures.json. validation-state.json has 9 VAL-CONTENT-* assertions that are NOT from the script output.

sharedStateObservations.observation: validation-worker skill Step 3 says 'For each lecture (1-10): Load data/processed/lectures.json, Calculate total character count, Count sections, Verify content > 100 chars'. This manual per-lecture check is not visible in the transcript skeleton. The transcript only shows npm run validate:content and npm run lint.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: SKILL.md Work Procedure Step 3 specifies per-lecture manual checks but transcript shows only two npm commands were run. Either the checks were done without logging, or the skill procedure wasn't followed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: L5 and L6 both have sourceFile='Lecture 6.pdf' in provenance but lecture5_full.txt and lecture6_full.txt are distinct files in data/raw/. The provenance should distinguish between them. This suggests either a copy-paste error or the source attribution is wrong.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: data/raw/ contains lecture5_full.txt (18956 chars) and lecture6_full.txt (8883 chars). L5 has 39401 chars, L6 has 8194 chars. L5's provenance says Lecture 6.pdf which doesn't match the extract-l5-full feature description.

summary: Feature passes validation - 956 assertions passed, lint passed with 4 non-blocking warnings. All 10 lectures present with 61 sections, all >5000 chars, no duplicates, no orphans, no placeholder text. One blocking issue: L5 provenance.sourceFile incorrectly points to Lecture 6.pdf instead of Lecture 5.pdf. One non-blocking issue: validate-content.ts doesn't actually validate lectures.json content (VAL-CONTENT-* assertions were manually added to state).
